param: EXIF FocalLength fed to the lensfun match (warp input TIFFs)
hypothesis: the community ptlens profile's paraxial error at the focal=70 calibrated entry shrinks when lensfun interpolates at the solved effective focal (67.8 mm, from the astrometric plate scale; a 50-70 blend), measured as the centre station moving toward the perpendicular-station floor on the 12-frame full-window instrument
instrument: qa_work/paraxial/run_paraxial.sh — 12 lights even-stride over the full 43-min window, production chain (master dark + validated sky flat, debayer, darktable lensdist distortion-only --icc-type SRGB, register -2pass, seqapplyreg -framing=min, stack rej 3 3 -norm=addscale)
measures: scripts/qa/star_stations.py (fixed drift-axis stations, Siril findstar fits; records qa_work/star_stations_parax_*.json) + scripts/qa/star_shape.py (Siril seqtilt)
verdict_reason: KILLED with numbers: the 67.8 key is WORSE at the centre station (majFWHM 5.42 / roundness 0.468 / n 487 vs control 4.88 / 0.516 / 570) and no better elsewhere (along+1300 4.57 vs 4.31) — the 50-70 interpolated model carries a larger paraxial error than the calibrated focal=70 entry. Side findings: the 12-frame instrument reproduces the centre band (centre 4.88 vs perp-1300 3.50 in the control arm), and seqtilt reads off-axis aberration 0.13-0.16 px on both arms — blind to the band, as the dead-end entry states.

param: lensfun distortion model source for the undistort stage
values: entry fitted from THIS unit's frames at infinity focus (Hugin cpfind + autooptimiser, star correspondences BETWEEN frames; a,b,c + d,e centre carried as a local lensfun override)
hypothesis: a model measured from our own frames removes the paraxial error the community profile carries (its a/b/c are fitted with the centre pinned at image centre, absorbing the calibrator copy's decentering into radial terms that do not transfer between units); centre-station majFWHM/roundness/count reach the perpendicular-station floor while mid/edge stations hold their corrected values
instrument: qa_work/paraxial/run_paraxial.sh — identical chain and frames as paraxial_focal_key; the only knob is the lensfun DB the warp sees (XDG_DATA_HOME tree), EXIF focal 70 in both arms
measures: scripts/qa/star_stations.py + scripts/qa/star_shape.py; fit-arm warp verified non-no-op by a one-frame probe before the run (darktable never fails loudly on a lens mismatch)

fit.tool: hugin-tools 2025.0.1: cpfind --fullscale on Siril-autostretched copies (geometry unchanged; 399 CPs over 53 pairs, baselines to ~910 px) -> cpclean (263 CPs survive) -> staged autooptimiser with v PINNED at the solved 30.35 deg (free-v collapses degenerate: v->0.93, a=98); rotations, then +abc, then +de
fit.curve_vs_community: agree at the crop corner (+41 px, delta 0.06 px); community overcorrects paraxial/mid by 2.4-3.9 px (r=200-1000) -- the measured eps(r) behind the band
fit.override: local lensfun DB copy (XDG_DATA_HOME tree qa_work/paraxial/xdg_fit), mil-nikon.xml focal=70 ptlens line replaced with fitted abc; all other entries preserved; d,e deliberately NOT applied (undocumented <center> element, sign unverified -- separately-bracketed knob, unnecessary so far)

verdict_reason: WIN on every recorded measure, instrument A/B: centre station majFWHM 4.88 -> 3.61 px (roundness 0.516 -> 0.636, n 570 -> 764), all 9 stations within 3.37-3.84 px (control spread 3.50-4.88), along+1300 4.31 -> 3.66; seqtilt stars 6462 -> 8175, truncated mean FWHM 3.19 -> 3.00 px, sensor tilt 0.30 -> 0.22. The band is removed and the whole frame sharpens. Production adoption pending the full-depth render + the user's eyes (aesthetic gate).

full_depth_168fr.vs_shipped_community_render: centre station 5.30 -> 3.67 px (roundness 0.480 -> 0.629, n 927 -> 1086), all-station spread 1.70 -> 0.52 px, seqtilt truncated-mean FWHM 3.27 -> 3.06 px, stars 11805 -> 12976, sensor tilt 0.51 -> 0.31 px (16% -> 10%), off-axis aberration 0.25 -> 0.25 (blind to the band either way)
full_depth_168fr.chain_note: one knob vs the shipped render (the lensfun DB the warp sees); frame selection 168-stride vs the shipped 169-stride-minus-lost-frame -- a ~1-frame phase difference, declared
full_depth_168fr.judgment: judge/FINAL_deepest_lensfun_fit.png vs judge/FINAL_deepest_lensfun.png, full-frame lossless PNG16, like encodings -- APPROVED on the user's eyes 2026-07-17; fitted entry installed via scripts/darktable/install_lens_model.sh, class builder scripts/stack/run_undistort_pipeline.sh

hypothesis: the union canvas (max) shows all sky any frame covered, at an acceptable quality cost confined to the coverage edges — judged against min's uniform full-depth crop for the quality-vs-quantity middle ground
instrument: identical 25 sub-stacks (369 frames, ratified cull, stripped-DB warp), compose-only re-run; one knob

measured.coverage_behavior: siril excludes uncovered inputs from the stack mean (edge sky levels match centre); never-covered union corners are exact zero
measured.solve: min: blind, logodds 298; max: blind grinds (coverage-seam false detections at the deflated threshold) — solved with --central=0.35 + position hint, logodds 225

judgment: judge/FULL369_min_spcc_linked.png vs judge/FULL369_max_spcc_linked.png, full-frame lossless PNG16, like encodings
verdict: open — the user's eyes; identified middle-ground option: max compose trimmed to a coverage threshold (keep regions >= N sub-stacks deep)

param: rejection at the group-composition final stack (across 25 sub-stacks)
values: rej 3 3 (winsorized — the pinned FRAME-level default carried over)
hypothesis: the frame-level rejection default transfers to the sub-stack compose
instrument: identical registered sub-stack sequence, one knob (the stack rejection); Siril isub both directions + seqtilt on both composes
verdict_reason: KILLED with numbers: rej 3 3 across the 25 clean means rewrote pixels by up to +3849/-2986 ADU on a ~140 ADU sky (diff sigma 12-27 ADU frame-wide, median 1 — damage concentrated in structured regions: carved star cores, dark streaks along steep gradients; the user's eyes found it on the stretched final). Whole-frame seqtilt is BLIND to it (stars 13903 vs 13784, FWHM 3.07 vs 3.10). Mechanism: sub-stack mutual scatter is ~sqrt(15) below per-frame noise, so a 3-sigma gate fires on sub-pixel registration differences, not outliers; satellites already reject in-group. Compose = plain mean (driver fixed); dead-ends entry added.

amendment: both arms re-composed with the mean-none final (the rejection dead-end above); judgment pair re-rendered: judge/FULL369_min_mean_spcc_linked.png vs judge/FULL369_max_mean_spcc_linked.png (+ judge/FULL369_min_mean_subsky1_PREVIEW.png — the designed subsky-1 background step previewed on the min arm for the corner-gradient question). Verdict remains open — the user's eyes.

param: -cc=dark cosmetic hot/cold-pixel correction in the undistort calibration
hypothesis: adding the -cc=dark the standard path already uses removes the drift-aligned background streaks
instrument: 40-frame even-stride subset (24 registered), un-warped calibrate->register->stack, one knob (-cc); Siril findstar + matched-stretch crops
verdict: quality WIN; streak hypothesis ruled out (streaks remain an OPEN gap)
verdict_reason: -cc=dark does NOT remove the streaks (drift-aligned walking noise; candidate levers dither/integration/denoise untried), but it is a real star/background quality win (rounder, sharper, ~500 hot-pixel candidates removed) and is the documented light-calibration default. Adopted + single-sourced in scripts/stack/calibrate_light.sh, guarded by check_calibrate.sh.

param: -cc=dark cosmetic hot/cold-pixel correction, on the full warped + SPCC production chain (re-derivation; the prior A/B was un-warped/un-SPCC'd/24-frame)
instrument: 168-frame even stride, identical fitted-model undistort chain both arms, one knob (-cc); OFF arm generated mechanically from run_undistort_pipeline.sh (guard stays green). Matched stretch: mtf computed once on ON (autostretch -linked), applied identically to both.
verdict: NULL on the render (streaks + shape + colour); tiny registration WIN for -cc
verdict_reason: NULL on every deliverable measure: seqtilt, drift-axis stations and SPCC K are identical off-vs-on, and the full-frame matched-stretch finals are IDENTICAL on the user's eyes (ON vs OFF). Only measurable effect: -cc recovered one marginal frame in registration (159 vs 158) by removing hot-pixel false detections. The walking-noise streak is NOT removed by -cc. Mechanism: discrete hot pixels are already sigma-rejected in integration AND removed by -cc; the drift streak is consistent with the electronic-shutter readout fixed-pattern (non-discrete -> -cc cannot touch it; EXIF confirms Shutter Mode: Electronic). -cc STAYS (documented light-cal default, single-sourced). Streak = OPEN gap (levers: ES-matched calibration, dither for future, post-hoc pattern/denoise), NOT a dead-end.

param: integration rejection algorithm (walking-noise Lever 2 + doctrine alignment for >50 subs)
hypothesis: GESD rejects the drift-walked hot/warm-pixel residual better than winsorized 3-sigma, thinning the walking-noise streaks (the drift puts each bad pixel at a different sky position per frame, so it is a per-sky-pixel minority a stronger reject can catch); AND it aligns the stack with Siril's documented rejection doctrine for >50 subs. Watch: over-rejection carving star cores/structure (finals). Caveat: if the streaks are sub-sigma ES readout banding, not discrete outliers, rejection cannot catch them -> NULL.
instrument: identical registered sequence (168-stride, -cc ON, fitted-model warp+register) stacked BOTH ways from the same r_lt frames (one knob = rejection); driver reject_work/run_undistort_reject_ab.sh = production script + REPO fix + a two-stack final block (-cc unchanged, guard green). Matched stretch (mtf from wins arm, applied to both).
verdict: open — the user's eyes on full-frame lossless finals (streak reduction vs core/structure integrity)
GESD ADOPTED (doctrine + safe); NULL on walking-noise streaks

measured.difference_wins_minus_gesd: median 1 ADU, sparse star-correlated outliers to ~1700 ADU; NO drift-aligned streak pattern, NO structured core/rip damage

verdict_reason: GESD is NULL on every deliverable measure (seqtilt shape, SPCC colour ~identical) and SAFE: the aligned-stack difference is sparse star-edge noise + a few outliers GESD caught that winsorized kept — no carved cores, no dark rips (unlike the sub-stack rejection dead-end). It does NOT reduce the walking-noise streaks (difference has no drift-aligned pattern), confirming the pre-registered caveat that the streaks are sub-sigma electronic-shutter fixed-pattern, not rejectable outliers. ADOPTED on the user's call: aligns the >50-sub stack with Siril's documented GESD doctrine, safe + NULL-on-quality. Walking-noise remains OPEN -> Lever 1 (matched-ES darks) / prevention (dither, mechanical shutter). Doctrine-driven rejection wiring in the builders is proposed, pending the user's go.
